name: What if my employer fires me after I report chemical exposure at work?
description: File retaliation claim—protected by law; doc everything.

mainEntity.name: What if my employer fires me after I report chemical exposure at work?

acceptedAnswer.text: File retaliation claim—protected by law; doc everything.
acceptedAnswer.description: **Utah and Idaho prohibit retaliation for filing workers' comp; you can claim wrongful termination or reinstatement.** Anti-retaliation laws protect reporting safety issues or injuries.[6] Key factors: timing of firing post-report, documentation of complaints, and witness statements. File retaliation charge with labor dept within 180-300 days; seek backpay/unemployment. Document all interactions; a lawyer handles dual claims effectively.
